what_it_does: This file consolidates the Round 2 review findings for the concurrent-cognition architecture, records corrected positions and revised decisions, identifies blockers by tier, and serves as a binding correction layer for the build sequence and design decisions.
observations: The verdict is that the architecture and serial-spine-plus-three-lanes build sequence are sound and unchanged.

H1 corrects the resource math: at util 0.49 the KV pool is ~70.6K tokens shared, so with short roles and bounded main context max_num_seqs=16 binds concurrency; only a full 64K main plus long roles collapses width to ~2-5.
H1 says swarm-heavy mode wants a higher-util brain config (~0.63), not a leaner one, and that mode should select the brain config as a first-class registry artifact.
H2 frames the remaining Tim decision as 'full declared logic' versus a bounded pure-expression grammar over resolved values, with arbitrary compute pushed to composed roles/nodes (the gate pattern).
H2 also enforces that rules are declared ASTs interpreted by a restricted evaluator, with per-rule readiness, fail-loud missing references, and no timeout-based readiness.

H3 details the chat() refactor as prologue-once, parameterized part-core, epilogue-once, and lists preservation traps including monkeypatch seams, differing return shapes, _chat_context side effects, and callers that must survive.
H4 flags four live 'swarm://' resolutions and three stale '32' assertions in the bodies as mechanically unbuildable and blocking G0.
H5 notes missing drift/self-description homes for net-new registries and that cognition.* was mislabelled as an emit-contract.
H6 lists under-specified net-new items (G5 triggers, THOUGHT_SHAPES schema, typed-lane destination, jury verdict-evaluator) that need HOW/schema specs before fan-out.
H7 asks for a scope note clarifying deterministic routing versus intentionally varied jury draws, and an unambiguous statement of the driver-located fork F2.
H8 records Tim's directive that per-mode model loadout is a configurable registry concern, not a fixed pick, and reports measured KV-pool numbers proving the Orpheus-vs-swarm tradeoff.
H8 instructs reuse of existing company CLI tooling (combos, groups, up, swap, config, gpu.py budget gate) rather than building a parallel switcher.
Net effect: G0 is buildable after H4, H1, and H7; fan-out is blocked on H5 and H6; one decision (H2) is Tim's.
standouts: H8: Tim's directive that mode selects the whole resident model loadout and must be configurable/experimentable via existing company CLI tooling, backed by measured KV-pool numbers.
H1: the corrected resource math showing swarm-mode needs higher util (~0.63), not leaner context, and that mode selects the brain config.
H2: the determinism/renderability boundary — rules as bounded pure-expression ASTs plus composition/gate pattern, surfaced to Tim rather than silently applied.
H4: the blocking finding that live 'swarm://' URLs and stale '32' assertions in the bodies make G0 mechanically unbuildable.
conventions: Uses structured H1-H8 headers with sub-bullets and bold emphasis for decisions and corrections.
Labels items by tier/severity (Tier 0 BLOCKING, Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3).
References criteria and guide sections by short IDs (C0.5, C1.2, G0, G5, etc.).
Includes explicit owner/decision markers ('Tim's to decide', 'Tim-directive').
Mixes measured numbers, design rationale, and action items in a single review fold.
concerns: Contains nested corrections and corrections-of-corrections (e.g., the '32' figure is corrected multiple times), which may confuse readers.
Some numerical claims are interdependent on a specific model/config and may drift if measurements or hardware assumptions change.
The 'binding correction layer' claim sits alongside an unresolved Tim decision (H2) and unspecified H5/H6 items, creating tension about how binding it is.
Heavy use of project-specific jargon and acronyms without inline definitions.
notes: This is the authoritative Round 2 correction memo: it confirms the concurrent-cognition architecture, fixes resource math and rule-determinism framing, lists G0 and fan-out blockers, and records Tim's directive that mode-driven model loadouts must stay configurable via existing company tooling.
questions: What are the exact file paths of the bodies still containing the four live 'swarm://' resolutions?
Where do the 'rhm_*' monkeypatch tests live, and what is the current chat() implementation they target?
What are the current post-edit contents of C0.5, C1.2, C1.7, G5, G8, and the new mode-to-loadout criterion?
Where in the codebase are the company CLI 'combos', 'groups', 'up', 'swap', 'config', and gpu.py budget gate implemented?
What is the precise bounded grammar AST schema proposed for rules in H2?
What is the definition of the THOUGHT_SHAPES schema and the typed-lane destination seam mentioned in H6?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the binding Round 2 correction layer, and the file does consolidate reviews, record corrected positions, list blockers, and surface a remaining decision — so it matches.
apparent_themes: Mode-driven configuration selects both brain config and full model loadout.
Resource-aware concurrency bounded by KV pool and max_num_seqs.
Determinism versus expressiveness/renderability in routing rules.
Registry-first design with self-description and drift acceptance.
Binding correction and tiered build sequencing (G0 versus fan-out).
Composition over arbitrary in-rule computation (the gate pattern).
intention_signals: It is trying to close the Round 2 review loop, prevent silent build breakage, enforce that mode selects both brain config and resident model loadout, and surface the one remaining design decision (rule grammar) to Tim instead of choosing it silently.
novelty: It acts as a 'fold' document that explicitly carries corrections-of-corrections, tiered blockers, measured KV-pool numbers tied to mode/loadout tradeoffs, and a directive to reuse existing company CLI tooling rather than build a parallel switcher.
contribution: This file contributes a consolidated, timestamped record of Round 2 corrections, measured resource constraints, a mode-to-brain-config and mode-to-loadout mapping, a rule-determinism framing, chat-refactor preservation traps, and a tiered blocker list that a fused system would keep as a build-prep reference.
summary_for_embedding: R2-FOLD.md is a binding Round 2 review correction document for the concurrent-cognition architecture, consolidating resource-math, chat-refactor, rule-determinism, and completeness findings into tiered blockers and revised positions.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to close the Round 2 review loop, correct stale numbers and schemes, record Tim's mode-to-loadout directive, and guide the G0 spike and fan-out sequencing.
